title: US: Replacing duration of status set to cause chaos
summary_en: The Trump administration’s proposed rule to replace ‘duration of status’ with fixed time limits on student and visitor visas is set to cause administrative chaos, leaders warn, anticipating the change in the coming weeks.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: [待翻译] US: Replacing duration of status set to cause chaos

title: UK Lords press ministers over sector visa challenges
summary_en: The UK minister of state for the Home Office has agreed to meet with sector leaders after concerns about incoming compliance rules and visa refusal rates were raised in the House of Lords.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: [待翻译] UK Lords press ministers over sector visa challenges

title: France hikes non-EU tuition fees, move dubbed “blow to university autonomy”
summary_en: France will require most non-EU students to pay higher tuition fees from 2026/27, as part of a wider push to reshape international recruitment under its 'Choose France for Higher Education' strategy.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: [待翻译] France hikes non-EU tuition fees, move dubbed “blow to university autonomy”

title: Dubai moves back to in-person teaching amid Iran war
summary_en: The private education sector in Dubai has taken steps towards resuming onsite education, after moving online amid regional conflict stemming from the Iran war.
author: Beth Kennedy
title_cn: [待翻译] Dubai moves back to in-person teaching amid Iran war

title: “Shift beyond big four evident” as Indian student demand holds up amid reset
summary_en: With higher education outbound numbers down over a third in two years, consultancies say a “clearly visible” shift sees Indian students look beyond traditional destinations as the market becomes more cautious.
author: Abhishek Nair
title_cn: [待翻译] “Shift beyond big four evident” as Indian student demand holds up amid reset

title: US politics drives international student self-censorship
summary_en: Nearly nine in 10 international students have reported being less willing to speak up in academic spaces or on social media about immigration, global conflict or US regulations, as experts warn of increasingly polarised classrooms.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: [待翻译] US politics drives international student self-censorship

title: Through the amber spyglass: a refraction of the new RAG rating
summary_en: Alex Lock and Mike Strong consider the unintended consequences of an amber rating from the UKVI's Basic Compliance Assessment (BCA) and how it should not be viewed as failure by universities.
author: Alex Lock and Mike Strong
title_cn: [待翻译] Through the amber spyglass: a refraction of the new RAG rating

title: Relief for some OPT applicants in “immigration limbo”
summary_en: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to lift an indefinite freeze on work permit authorisations, including OPT, for a group of international students from travel ban countries.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: [待翻译] Relief for some OPT applicants in “immigration limbo”

title: Canada’s language sector looks to joint pathways to rebuild trust and growth
summary_en: Canada’s language education sector has been hit hard by a combination of pandemic disruption, policy change and closures, but a new Joint Pathway Program is emerging as a focal point for renewal.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: [待翻译] Canada’s language sector looks to joint pathways to rebuild trust and growth

title: Scholarship targets study abroad gap for US community colleges
summary_en: A new scholarship has launched to expand access to study abroad opportunities to community college students in the US – a population starkly underrepresented in global learning initiatives.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: [待翻译] Scholarship targets study abroad gap for US community colleges

title: How many international students are studying in Australia?
summary_en: The PIE compiles the most recent data on the number of international students in Australia, including those in the post-graduate work stream, along with insights into the leading source markets.
author: Charlotte Greenstein
title_cn: [待翻译] How many international students are studying in Australia?

title: Universities face “rollercoaster” decade as AI tests their value
summary_en: AI and rapid technological change are forcing universities to rethink their very purpose, global university leaders have warned, as they discuss what value higher education offers in a world where machines "compete with our brain".
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: [待翻译] Universities face “rollercoaster” decade as AI tests their value

title: DAAD shifts Indian focus beyond student mobility
summary_en: In a conversation with The PIE, senior DAAD officials highlighted a shift in India strategy towards long-term partnerships and research, while tackling one-sided mobility and access gaps.
author: Abhishek Nair
title_cn: [待翻译] DAAD shifts Indian focus beyond student mobility

title: Demand outstrips capacity at South Africa’s universities, warns UJ vice-chancellor
summary_en: South Africa’s public universities are facing a "profound contradiction" as demand for higher education surges beyond capacity, University of Johannesburg vice‑chancellor and principal Letlhokwa Mpedi has warned.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: [待翻译] Demand outstrips capacity at South Africa’s universities, warns UJ vice-chancellor
